fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Rhode Island Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Rhode Island - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity rhode island, how to transfer dietitian license to rhode island, dietitian compact rhode island, rhode island dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Dietetics Practice
board.mailing_address:
Center for Professional Licensing, Room 104, 3 Capitol Hill
Providence, RI 02908

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (expires Nov 1 of odd years) + 20 CE hours

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The credential to practice and provide medical nutrition therapy in Rhode Island, overseen by the Board of Dietetics Practice.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

steps.title: Submit the Application and Fee
steps.description: Complete, sign, and notarize the official application. Submit it to the Department of Health with the $75 fee (payable to the RI General Treasurer) and a 2x3 portrait photograph.

steps.title: Complete Background Check & Verifications
steps.description: Obtain an original Bureau of Criminal Identification (BCI) report from the Attorney General's Office. Ensure CDR verification is sent directly to the Board.

documents.detail: Application form with a notarized signature and a recent 2x3 head-and-shoulders photograph.
Proof of active registration sent directly from the CDR to the Board.
An original BCI report from the Rhode Island Attorney General's Office.

provisional_text: Rhode Island offers a temporary permit for individuals who have completed their education and supervised practice but are awaiting the results of the CDR exam. A temporary permit holder may practice under supervision for 90 days and use the title 'graduate dietitian/nutritionist'.
reciprocity_text: Rhode Island offers licensure by endorsement for dietitians/nutritionists actively licensed in other states, provided the requirements of the other state are equivalent to Rhode Island's. Applying via endorsement requires submitting the application, fee, and verification of all out-of-state licenses.

faqs.question: How much does a Rhode Island dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial cost is a $75 application fee, which does not include the cost of the BCI background check.

faqs.question: When does the Rhode Island license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Rhode Island LDN licenses expire on November 1st of odd-numbered years. The biennial renewal fee is $170.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island use the title 'Nutritionist'?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rhode Island uses the combined title of Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN) to license both professionals under one standard for medical nutrition therapy.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 5 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Rhode Island offers licensure by endorsement for dietitians/nutritionists actively licensed in other states, provided the requirements of the other state are equivalent to Rhode Island's. Applying via endorsement requires submitting the application, fee, and verification of all out-of-state licenses.
